message:
fix(lock): replace exponential backoff polling with true blocking flock for write lock acquisition

The write lock acquisition used a busy-wait loop with exponential backoff
(50ms to 800ms, 12 attempts, ~8s total) that polled try_lock() repeatedly.
This wasted CPU during contention and could still fail after the backoff
budget was exhausted, silently proceeding without the lock.

- Keep the fast-path try_lock() for the common uncontended case (no change)
- Replace the polling loop with a single blocking file.lock() call that uses
  the OS-level flock(LOCK_EX) on Linux / fcntl on macOS — a true wait that
  releases immediately when the holder drops, with zero CPU cost while waiting
- Add debug logging when entering the blocking wait path
- On lock failure, log and return None (same as before) so the SQLite retry
  loop provides the secondary safety net

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
